Welcome to Employee Ownership
A Letter from Your ESOP Trustee
Company
{{companyName}}
Trustee
{{trusteeName}}
Announcement Date
{{announcementDate}}
Employee Count
{{employeeCount}}
§ 01Who I Am

My name is {{trusteeName}}, and I serve as the independent trustee of the {{companyName}} Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP) Trust. I want to take a moment to introduce myself and explain what today means for you.

As your ESOP trustee, I have a legal obligation under federal law — the Employee Retirement Income Security Act (ERISA) — to act solely in the best interests of you and your fellow employee-owners. I do not work for the company's former owner, management, or any outside investor. I work for the trust, and the trust exists to benefit you.

My role is to make sure the ESOP is managed prudently, that the company stock is valued fairly each year, and that your ownership rights are protected.

§ 02What Happened Today

Today, {{companyName}} became a 100% employee-owned company. The previous owner sold their shares to the ESOP trust — a special kind of retirement plan that now holds the company's stock on behalf of all eligible employees.

You are now a beneficial owner of {{companyName}}.

This happened at no cost to you. You did not need to invest any of your own money. The ESOP trust purchased the company using financing that the company will repay over time — similar to how a company might take out a loan to grow its business.

Nothing about your daily work, your manager, or your job responsibilities changes as a result of today's announcement.

§ 03What This Means for You

As an employee-owner, you will accumulate shares in the ESOP trust over time. Here is what that means in practical terms:

A.Ownership stake that grows. Each year, the company contributes to the ESOP on your behalf. The longer you work here, the more shares you accumulate.
B.Retirement benefit at no cost. Your ESOP account is a retirement benefit — like a 401(k) — except you do not contribute any of your own paycheck. The company funds it entirely.
C.Annual account statements. Each year, you will receive a statement showing how many shares you hold and what they are worth, based on an independent valuation.
D.Potential for meaningful retirement wealth. As the company performs well and pays down the acquisition debt, the value of your shares can grow significantly over time. Many employee-owners in ESOP companies build substantial retirement savings through their ownership stake.
§ 04Your Rights and Protections

Federal law provides strong protections for ESOP participants. Here are the most important ones:

A.ERISA protections. The Employee Retirement Income Security Act is the same federal law that protects 401(k) and pension plans. Your ESOP account is covered by these same protections.
B.Independent trustee oversight. As your trustee, I am required by law to act in your interest — not the company's, not management's, and not any former owner's.
C.Annual independent valuation. Each year, an independent appraiser determines the fair market value of the company stock. This ensures your account is valued accurately and fairly.
D.Right to information. You have the right to receive plan documents, annual reports, and information about your account. If you have questions, you can ask — and you are entitled to answers.
E.Diversification rights. Beginning at age 55 with at least 10 years of participation, you have the right to diversify a portion of your ESOP account into other investments.
F.Distribution rights. When you leave the company — whether through retirement, job change, or other reasons — you are entitled to receive the value of your vested ESOP account, paid out over a period defined by the plan.
§ 05What Does NOT Change

I want to be clear about this: your day-to-day work life is not changing.

✓Your job is the same. Your position, duties, and reporting structure are unchanged.
✓Your manager is the same. The people you work with and report to are not changing.
✓Your pay is the same. Your salary, wages, and regular compensation are unaffected.
✓Your benefits are the same. Health insurance, PTO, and other benefits continue as before.
✓The company is the same. {{companyName}} keeps its name, its customers, and its independence.

The ESOP is an additional benefit on top of everything you already have. It does not replace or reduce anything.

§ 06What Happens Next

Over the coming weeks, you will receive more information about the ESOP and what it means for you:

A.Welcome packet. You will receive a detailed welcome packet with your enrollment information, plan summary, and answers to common questions.
B.ESOP committee. The company will form an employee-owner committee to help communicate about the ESOP, gather feedback, and represent employee interests.
C.Annual meeting. Each year, the company will hold a meeting to update all employee-owners on company performance and ESOP account values.
D.Questions are welcome. If you have questions at any time, you can reach out to your HR department, your manager, or contact me directly through the plan administrator. No question is too small.
§ 07My Commitment to You

As your independent trustee, I want you to know what I commit to:

My fiduciary promise: I will protect your interests as employee-owners. I will ensure the company stock is valued fairly and independently each year. I will be transparent about how the trust is managed. And I will be available — through the plan administrator — if you ever have concerns about the ESOP or your account.

You are now part of something meaningful. Employee ownership is not just a financial benefit — it is a different way of working together. Research consistently shows that employee-owned companies are more productive, more resilient, and provide greater financial security for their workers.

Welcome to ownership.

—{{trusteeName}}
{{trusteeName}}
Independent ESOP Trustee
{{trusteeFirm}}
